statement: The first rewrite should use exactly two top-level workflows, RunWorkflow and WindowWorkflow, no child workflows initially, and a mixed control plane where Signals handle fire-and-forget nudges while Updates are reserved for validated or acknowledged operator actions.
rationale: A small workflow surface reduces operational and agent complexity, while a mixed Signals/Updates model maps better to real control semantics than a strict signals-only approach.
keyAssumptions: Initial run and window fanout can be handled with bounded parallelism and continue-as-new before child workflows become necessary
Temporal Updates are available and acceptable in the chosen self-hosted environment when acknowledgement is required
validationCriteria: pause_after, pause, resume, cancel, and bounded repair all map cleanly onto Temporal control primitives without extra queue machinery
The workflow histories remain manageable under realistic run and window sizes
critiquePoints: Overusing Updates could complicate deployment and compatibility if feature assumptions are wrong
questions: Which operator actions require acknowledgement versus best-effort delivery?
